title: lepigonum depauperatum
intro: Lepigonum depauperatum is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the 'poor man's lemon' due to its citrus-like fragrance and flavor. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are thought to be attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. Lepigonum depauperatum has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and fruits are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
